rationale: The cited paper is used in the introduction and literature review as core prior evidence that customers acquired via WOM/referrals are more valuable than those acquired through other marketing channels. This is not a stray mention; it helps establish the baseline result that the current paper explicitly builds on when arguing that prior work focused mainly on direct purchase/activity value. The citing paper then adds an additional downstream value component (referred customers make more referrals), positioning its contribution as a step beyond that earlier acquisition-channel value evidence.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Villanueva et al. (2008)'s acquisition-channel customer value findings by adding a new downstream benefit: referred customers' higher propensity to generate further referrals. It extends the prior value comparison from direct customer activity to broader social/referral-driven value.
